note: A message from the management greets you when the game loads
See what changed — a What's New page in the About screen
Level picker shows the record holder in Fly mode too
The racing ghost stays hidden until your run actually starts
The Caves is now distance-scored like the other levels
The racing ghost carries its pilot's callsign
World records show who holds them — "by <pilot>" in the HUD and level picker
The in-flight BEST and the racing ghost are the global world record
Anonymous play stats with an opt-in returning-player id (privacy note in About)
Today / This week boards show a countdown to their reset
Dates and times are shown in your region's own format
Submitting a score is always an explicit choice — submit dialog on every crash
Better boards: level records in the picker, timestamps, instant cached paint, scrolling
Simpler menu: level choice is a step inside Fly and High scores
Online high scores: global Today / This week / All time boards with pilot callsigns
Replay controls auto-hide like a video player — tap to bring them back
Replay transport: pause, scrub, frame-step and ¼×–4× playback speed
Runs start on your first input — take your time on the pad
HUD cleanup: big distance readout, fuel/hull icons, telemetry behind a Debug HUD setting
New neon-arcade game menu with the animated ship
The touch stick now spawns anywhere on screen
Sound setting for the engine and crash audio (off by default)
High scores with watchable replays, plus a race-the-best-run ghost
Three levels — The Expanse, The Glide and The Caves — with a level picker
The touch stick moved into the game canvas — smoother one-handed flight
Race a translucent ghost of your previous run
Crash dialog with an instant replay of the run
Invert stick setting — pull back to pitch up, like a flight stick
One-handed flight: holding the stick fires the main engine
The stick commands nose direction — the ship rotates to where you push
Gentler difficulty: graduated hull damage and softer handling
One zoom level across portrait and landscape on phones
"New build available" reload toast when a fresh deploy lands
Landing pads: land to score and refuel
Sharp native-resolution rendering on high-DPI screens
Engine rumble and crash boom — procedurally synthesised audio
Fuel system with a HUD gauge
Crashes: impact detection, explosion and respawn
Vertical shafts connect stacked cave layers — fly up and down forever
Bluetooth/USB game controller support
The real ship from the original Flash game, extracted from the SWF
Low-poly faceted crystal-rock look for walls and boulders
Boulders to navigate around in the cave
True per-pixel radial lighting around the ship
Install to your home screen (PWA support)
Minimap of the cave and your viewport
Thruster particle effects for the main engine and RCS
An infinite scrolling cave
Camera follow with a parallax starfield
First touch controls for mobile
Rotation controls and directional thrust
Pegasus flies in the browser — compiled to WebAssembly
